sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious File Creation In Uncommon AppData Folder
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of suspicious files and folders inside the user's AppData folder but not inside any of the common and well known directories (Local, Romaing, LocalLow). This method could be used as a method to bypass detection who exclude the AppData folder in fear of FPs
Detects the execution of powershell, a WebClient object creation and the invocation of DownloadFile in a single command line

crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Felix Bilstein - yara-signator at cocacoding dot com

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This binary is a malware dropper compiled using PureBasic (indicated by internal strings such as 'PB_WindowID' and 'PB_PostEventMessage'). Its primary malicious behavior involves extracting data from its resource section, decrypting/decompressing it using a custom routine (sub_402000), and writing it to a temporary file via 'GetTempFileNameA'. The dropper specifically renames this file to have a '.vbs' extension using 'PathRenameExtensionA' and executes the resulting VBScript using 'ShellExecuteExA' with the 'open' verb. Furthermore, the sample performs environment fingerprinting through OS version checks ('GetVersionExA') and identifies system paths to facilitate payload execution. These behaviors—dropping and executing scripts from temp directories—are classic indicators of secondary stage malware delivery.

attributes.magic: PE32 executable for MS Windows (GUI) Intel 80386 32-bit

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious File Creation In Uncommon AppData Folder
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of suspicious files and folders inside the user's AppData folder but not inside any of the common and well known directories (Local, Romaing, LocalLow). This method could be used as a method to bypass detection who exclude the AppData folder in fear of FPs
Detects the execution of powershell, a WebClient object creation and the invocation of DownloadFile in a single command line

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)
